comments.body:
## Summary of Changes Hello @UtakataKyosui, I'm Gemini Code Assist[^1]! I'm currently reviewing this pull request and will post my feedback shortly. In the meantime, here's a summary to help you and other reviewers quickly get up to speed! This pull request focuses on a suite of usability and performance enhancements for the application. It integrates WebAssembly for efficient image processing, upgrades password security with a robust hashing algorithm, and introduces a user-friendly dark/light theme. Additionally, significant strides have been made in improving overall application accessibility, ensuring a more inclusive user experience. The underlying data model has also been extended to support new features. ### Highlights * **WASM Image Compression**: Implemented WebAssembly (WASM) based image compression and resizing using `@silvia-odwyer/photon` for item images, improving performance during uploads and reducing storage size. A visual preview and compression status are provided in the item form. * **Enhanced Password Security**: Migrated password hashing and verification from `Bun.password` to `hash-wasm` using the Argon2id algorithm, significantly strengthening user authentication security. * **Dark/Light Theme Toggle**: Introduced a new theme provider and toggle component, allowing users to switch between dark and light modes. The theme preference is persisted locally, and a server-side script prevents Flash of Unstyled Content (FOUC). * **Accessibility Improvements**: Applied various accessibility fixes across forms (Box, Category, Item, Storage, Login) by adding `aria-describedby` attributes to forms, `for` attributes to labels, and `id` attributes to select elements, enhancing usability for assistive technologies. Required attributes were also added to login fields. * **New Skill Documentation**: Added comprehensive skill documents outlining best practices for HTML accessibility (semantic HTML, WAI-ARIA, Markuplint) and WebAssembly performance optimization (when to use WASM, recommended libraries, best practices). * **Database Schema Update**: The `items` table in the database schema was updated to include an optional `image` text column, enabling storage of compressed image data. 🧠 **New Feature in Public Preview:** You can now enable **Memory** to help **Gemini Code Assist** learn from your team's feedback. This makes future code reviews more consistent and personalized to your project's style. **Click [here](https://codeassist.google/code-review/login) to enable Memory in your admin console.**
Changelog * **.agent/skills/html-accessibility-best-practices/SKILL.md** * Added new skill documentation detailing HTML best practices, WAI-ARIA usage, and Markuplint for accessibility validation. * **.agent/skills/wasm-performance-optimization/SKILL.md** * Added new skill documentation providing guidelines for WebAssembly (WASM) performance optimization, including use cases and recommended libraries. * **bun.lock** * Updated dependencies to include `@silvia-odwyer/photon` and `hash-wasm`. * **drizzle/migrations/0004_many_unus.sql** * Added a new migration to include an `image` text column in the `items` table. * **drizzle/migrations/meta/0004_snapshot.json** * Generated a new Drizzle migration snapshot reflecting the database schema changes. * **drizzle/migrations/meta/_journal.json** * Updated the Drizzle migration journal to record the new migration. * **drizzle/schema.ts** * Added an optional `image` text column to the `Items` schema definition. * **package.json** * Added `@silvia-odwyer/photon` and `hash-wasm` to project dependencies. * **panda.config.ts** * Defined a new `canvas` semantic color token for theming, supporting light and dark modes. * **src/api/item/server.ts** * Modified `createItem` and `updateItem` functions to store the `image` field from form data. * **src/api/server.ts** * Replaced `Bun.password` with `hash-wasm` (Argon2id) for password hashing and verification. * Added a `getRandomSalt` utility function for password hashing. * Modified `getUser` to explicitly redirect to `/login` if a user is not found after logout. * **src/app.tsx** * Integrated `ThemeProvider` and `ThemeToggle` for global theme management. * Updated navigation styling to be sticky and adjusted element types from `div` to `ul` for semantic correctness. * Applied dark mode styling to the navigation bar. * **src/components/box/BoxForm.tsx** * Added `aria-describedby` to the form for error messages. * Improved accessibility of the storage select input by adding `for` to the label and an `id` to the select element. * Cast submission result to `Error` type for consistent error message handling. * **src/components/category/CategoryForm.tsx** * Added `aria-describedby` to the form for error messages. * Cast submission result to `Error` type for consistent error message handling. * **src/components/item/ItemForm.tsx** * Updated `ItemFormProps` to include an optional `image` field. * Implemented WASM-based image compression and resizing using `@silvia-odwyer/photon` for uploaded images. * Added a file input for image selection with a preview and compression status indicator. * Disabled the submit button during image compression. * Added `aria-describedby` to the form for error messages. * Cast submission result to `Error` type for consistent error message handling. * **src/components/storage/StorageForm.tsx** * Added `aria-describedby` to the form for error messages. * Cast submission result to `Error` type for consistent error message handling. * **src/components/theme-provider.tsx** * Added a new component to provide theme context and manage color mode state (light/dark). * **src/components/ui/theme-toggle.tsx** * Added a new UI component for toggling between light and dark themes, using `lucide-solid` icons. * **src/entry-server.tsx** * Added an inline script to `head` for initial theme setting during server-side rendering, preventing FOUC. * **src/routes/login.tsx** * Added `aria-describedby` to the login form for error messages. * Added `required` attributes to username and password input fields. * Cast submission result to `Error` type for consistent error message handling. * **src/theme/conditions.ts** * Updated `light` condition and added `dark` condition to support theme switching via `data-theme` attribute.
Activity * No specific human activity (comments, reviews, progress) was provided in the context for this pull request.
Using Gemini Code Assist
The full guide for Gemini Code Assist can be found on our [documentation page](https://developers.google.com/gemini-code-assist/docs/review-github-code), here are some quick tips. Invoking Gemini You can request assistance from Gemini at any point by creating a comment using either `/gemini ` or `@gemini-code-assist `. Below is a summary of the supported commands on the current page. Feature | Command | Description --- | --- | --- Code Review | `/gemini review` | Performs a code review for the current pull request in its current state. Pull Request Summary | `/gemini summary` | Provides a summary of the current pull request in its current state. Comment | @gemini-code-assist | Responds in comments when explicitly tagged, both in pull request comments and review comments. Help | `/gemini help` | Displays a list of available commands. Customization To customize Gemini Code Assist for GitHub experience, repository maintainers can create a configuration file and/or provide a custom code review style guide (such as PEP-8 for Python) by creating and adding files to a `.gemini/` folder in the base of the repository. Detailed instructions can be found [here](https://developers.google.com/gemini-code-assist/docs/customize-gemini-behavior-github). Limitations & Feedback Gemini Code Assist may make mistakes. Please leave feedback on any instances where its feedback is incorrect or counter productive. You can react with :thumbsup: and :thumbsdown: on @gemini-code-assist comments. If you're interested in giving your feedback about your experience with Gemini Code Assist for Github and other Google products, sign up [here](https://google.qualtrics.com/jfe/form/SV_2cyuGuTWsEw84yG). You can also get AI-powered code generation, chat, as well as code reviews directly in the IDE at no cost with the [Gemini Code Assist IDE Extension](https://cloud.google.com/products/gemini/code-assist).
[^1]: Review the [Privacy Notices](https://policies.google.com/privacy), [Generative AI Prohibited Use Policy](https://policies.google.com/terms/generative-ai/use-policy), [Terms of Service](https://policies.google.com/terms), and learn how to configure Gemini Code Assist in GitHub [here](https://developers.google.com/gemini-code-assist/docs/customize-gemini-behavior-github). Gemini can make mistakes, so double check it and [use code with caution](https://support.google.com/legal/answer/13505487).

reviews.body:
## Code Review

This pull request introduces several significant improvements focused on usability and performance. It adds accessibility features across various forms, such as using `aria-describedby` for error messages and correctly associating labels with inputs. A major highlight is the integration of WebAssembly (WASM) for performance-critical tasks: client-side image compression using `@silvia-odwyer/photon` and more secure, portable password hashing with `hash-wasm`. The PR also implements a dark mode theme provider with a toggle, improving user experience. My review focuses on a critical security vulnerability in the password hashing implementation and a high-priority performance improvement for the image handling logic. Overall, these are great enhancements to the application.
